title: Yellow Skin in Newborn - Neonatal Jaundice
chief_complaint: Full-term newborn (39 weeks), now Day 3 of life, with yellow discoloration of skin and eyes noticed by mother. Breastfeeding well. ABO compatible.

physical_exam.skin: Jaundice to abdomen (Kramer zone 3), no pallor, no petechiae

hidden_data.bilirubin: Total Bilirubin: 16.5 mg/dL, Direct: 0.4 mg/dL (indirect hyperbilirubinemia)
hidden_data.cbc: Hb: 16.5, Reticulocytes: 4%, blood type B+ (mother O+)

key_learning_points: Plot on Bhutani nomogram for risk
Check bilirubin levels based on age in hours

patient_responses.feeding: He breastfeeds every 2-3 hours, good latch

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Physiologic jaundice in a healthy term infant.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is physiologic neonatal jaundice. The indirect hyperbilirubinemia without hemolysis is characteristic.
feedback.attending_note: Physiologic jaundice: Plot bilirubin on Bhutani nomogram. Phototherapy if above threshold. Encourage frequent breastfeeding (8-12x/day). Recheck bilirubin 12-24h after starting phototherapy. Most cases resolve without intervention.
